ui.endingTitle: 大家理解並喜歡上台灣夜市文化
ui.endingDesc: 凌晨兩點，鹹酥雞與九層塔的香味，讓來自世界各地的旅人都有了共同的記憶。
ui.mainLabel: 夜市鹹酥雞遊戲主畫面
ui.canvasLabel: 橫向卷軸遊戲畫布：夜市場景與旅人

npc_iceland.title: 冰島旅人
lines: 我們半夜只有極光，沒有炸雞……
原來大家是來吃這個的，難怪這麼多人！

npc_mongolia.title: 蒙古旅人
lines: 草原上沒有這樣的攤子。
半夜還能買到熱的，好厲害。

npc_bhutan.title: 不丹旅人
lines: 幸福指數裡有鹹酥雞嗎？
聞起來很幸福。

npc_madagascar.title: 馬達加斯加旅人
lines: 跟我們那裡的夜市完全不一樣。
這個香味我記住了！

npc_paraguay.title: 巴拉圭旅人
lines: 南美也愛宵夜，但沒有九層塔。
我想試試看加九層塔的。

npc_slovenia.title: 斯洛維尼亞旅人
lines: 歐洲半夜只有酒吧。
這裡的宵夜文化太有趣了。

npc_namibia.title: 納米比亞旅人
lines: 沙漠晚上只有星空與營火。
沒想到城市裡也有這種溫暖。

npc_albania.title: 阿爾巴尼亞旅人
lines: 我們也有夜市，但沒有這種香味。
蒜頭跟九層塔是秘密嗎？

npc_greenland.title: 格陵蘭旅人
lines: 冰天雪地裡熱騰騰的炸物很吸引人。
我想買一袋帶回去。

npc_bolivia.title: 玻利維亞旅人
lines: 高海拔餓了也想來一袋。
台灣人半夜不睡覺都在吃這個？

obj_basil.title: 九層塔
lines: 鹹酥雞的靈魂配角之一。

obj_garlic.title: 蒜頭
lines: 切碎加進去，香味加倍。

obj_food_bag.title: 食物袋
lines: 剛炸好的鹹酥雞，裝在袋子裡還熱熱的。

stall.title: 鹹酥雞攤
lines: 老闆：要什麼？
（點「關閉」後若已滿足條件，可再點攤位購買）

stall_buy.title: 鹹酥雞攤
lines: 老闆：好，一袋鹹酥雞！
你買到了一袋鹹酥雞。

ui.endingTitle: Everyone understands and loves Taiwan’s night market culture
ui.endingDesc: At 2 a.m., the smell of fried chicken and basil gives travelers from around the world a shared memory.
ui.canvasLabel: Side-scrolling game canvas: night market and travelers

npc_iceland.title: Traveler from Iceland
lines: At midnight we only have the northern lights, not fried chicken…
So everyone comes for this—no wonder it’s so crowded!

npc_mongolia.title: Traveler from Mongolia
lines: We don’t have stalls like this on the grassland.
Getting something hot this late at night is amazing.

npc_bhutan.title: Traveler from Bhutan
lines: Is fried chicken part of the happiness index?
It smells like happiness.

npc_madagascar.title: Traveler from Madagascar
lines: Nothing like the night markets back home.
I’ll remember this smell.

npc_paraguay.title: Traveler from Paraguay
lines: South America loves late-night food too, but we don’t have basil.
I’d like to try it with basil.

npc_slovenia.title: Traveler from Slovenia
lines: In Europe at midnight there are only bars.
This late-night food culture is so interesting.

npc_namibia.title: Traveler from Namibia
lines: In the desert at night there’s only the stars and a campfire.
I didn’t expect this kind of warmth in the city.

npc_albania.title: Traveler from Albania
lines: We have night markets too, but not this smell.
Are garlic and basil the secret?

npc_greenland.title: Traveler from Greenland
lines: Hot fried food in the ice and snow is very appealing.
I want to buy a bag to take back.

npc_bolivia.title: Traveler from Bolivia
lines: When you’re hungry at high altitude you want a bag too.
Do people in Taiwan stay up late just to eat this?

obj_basil.title: Basil
lines: One of the soul sidekicks of fried chicken.

obj_garlic.title: Garlic
lines: Chopped and added in, the smell doubles.

obj_food_bag.title: Food bag
lines: Freshly fried chicken, still hot in the bag.

stall.title: Fried chicken stall
lines: Vendor: What would you like?
(Close this, then tap the stall again to buy if you’ve met the condition.)

stall_buy.title: Fried chicken stall
lines: Vendor: One bag of fried chicken, coming up!
You bought a bag of fried chicken.
